text.title: Jenkins Declarative Pipelines Core Rules
text.summary: ≥5 testable rules grounding the jenkins-pipelines methodology, each paired with rationale and source reference.

rule.statement: Default to declarative syntax; use scripted (node block) only when dynamic logic such as runtime service detection is required.
rule.rationale: Declarative pipelines are linter-checkable and version-comparable; scripted is for the residual 10% that needs it.

rule.statement: Every pipeline includes options { timeout, timestamps, buildDiscarder, disableConcurrentBuilds } sized to the workload.
rule.rationale: Without these four options pipelines either run forever, lose logs, fill disk or stomp on each other.

rule.statement: Run independent stages — unit tests, integration tests, security scans — inside parallel { } blocks.
rule.rationale: Serial execution of independent stages is the single largest wasted-time source in legacy Jenkinsfiles.

rule.statement: Gate production deployments with input { } requiring named approvers and restrict the stage with when { branch 'main' }.
rule.rationale: Unguarded prod deploy on feature branches is the recurring incident root cause — input + when is the safe pair.

rule.statement: Pin shared-library versions explicitly via @Library('lib@vX.Y.Z'); never use @main or @HEAD in production pipelines.
rule.rationale: @main pulls live tip on every build — the same supply-chain failure mode as floating action tags.

rule.statement: If preconditions in `Applies If` are not all true, do NOT run this methodology — return early with reason.
rule.rationale: Decision tree's negative branch must terminate cleanly without partial output.

rule.statement: If preconditions hold AND scope is explicit, run the methodology end-to-end and produce the canonical artefact.
rule.rationale: Decision tree positive-branch terminus; aliases the apply-end-to-end verdict.
